_source: Gen 2 Compendiums/LINEAR MODELLING.pdf, TASC MTG215123 Module 2 Topic 2. NOTE: this compendium covers lines, intersection and linear modelling but NOT piecewise-linear/step graphs (a TASC requirement) — that gap is flagged to the teacher, not filled from general knowledge. Answer keys verified by tools/verify_answers.py.

unit.title: Linear Equations & Graphs
unit.subtitle: Module 2 · Topic 2
unit.description: Straight-line graphs and their equations: gradient, intercepts, sketching, the point of intersection of two lines, and using lines of best fit to model data and make predictions. Criteria C6 and C1. (Piecewise-linear and step graphs are taught in class — not in this compendium.)

lessons.title: The gradient of a line
lessons.overview: Measuring how steeply a line slopes.

sections.title: Gradient formula
sections.html:
The gradient measures how steeply a line slopes:

gradient = rise ÷ run = (y₂ − y₁) ÷ (x₂ − x₁)

  • Positive gradient → slopes up left-to-right.
  • Negative gradient → slopes down left-to-right.
  • Zero gradient → horizontal line.
  • The larger the size of the gradient, the steeper the line.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.html: The gradient between (1, 2) and (4, 11) is (11 − 2) ÷ (4 − 1) = 9 ÷ 3 = 3. Between (−5, 6) and (1, −6) it is (−6 − 6) ÷ (1 − −5) = −12 ÷ 6 = −2 (a downward slope).

lessons.title: The equation of a straight line
lessons.overview: Reading gradient and intercepts from y = ax + b.

sections.title: y = ax + b
sections.html:
A straight line can be written y = ax + b, where a is the gradient and b is the y-intercept (the value of y when x = 0).

  • y = 3x − 2: gradient 3, y-intercept −2.
  • y = −x + 4: gradient −1, y-intercept 4.
  • y = 6: gradient 0, y-intercept 6.

sections.title: Intercepts and sketching
sections.html: You only need two points to draw a line. The y-intercept is b. The x-intercept is where the line cuts the x-axis — set y = 0 and solve. For y = 4x + 8: 0 = 4x + 8, so x = −2. Plot (0, 8) and (−2, 0) and join them.

lessons.title: The intersection of two lines
lessons.overview: Solving two linear equations at once.

sections.title: Point of intersection
sections.html:
Two lines cross where they share the same x and y — the point of intersection, found by solving them simultaneously. When both are in the form y = ax + b, equate the right-hand sides.

Find where y = 4x + 9 meets y = −2x − 3: set 4x + 9 = −2x − 3, giving 6x = −12, so x = −2. Substitute back: y = 4(−2) + 9 = 1. They meet at (−2, 1).

sections.title: Break-even problems
sections.html: Intersections model break-even points, where a cost line and a revenue line cross — below it you make a loss, above it a profit.

lessons.title: Linear modelling & predictions
lessons.overview: Fitting a line to data and using it to predict.

sections.title: Scatter plots and lines of best fit
sections.html: A scatter plot shows paired data as points. If the points roughly follow a straight-line trend, a line of best fit (by eye or by calculator regression) summarises the relationship and lets us make predictions.

sections.title: Interpolation, extrapolation & reliability
sections.html: Predicting within the range of the data is interpolation and is generally reliable. Predicting outside the data range is extrapolation and is far less reliable, because the trend may not continue.

quizzes.title: Quiz: Gradient & equations of lines
quizzes.overview: Gradients, intercepts and reading straight-line equations. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: Find the gradient of the line through (1, 2) and (4, 11).
Find the gradient of the line through (−5, 6) and (1, −6).
State the y-intercept of the line y = 3x − 2.
questions.explain: In y = ax + b, b is the y-intercept, so it is −2.

questions.prompt: Find the x-intercept of the line y = 4x + 8 (the value of x when y = 0).
For the line y = 2x − 5, find y when x = 3.
A horizontal line has a gradient of:
questions.explain: A horizontal line has zero rise, so its gradient is 0.

questions.prompt: In the equation y = ax + b, the value of a represents the:
questions.explain: a is the gradient; b is the y-intercept.

quizzes.title: Quiz: Intersections & linear modelling
quizzes.overview: Points of intersection and predicting from a line of best fit. Unlimited attempts.

questions.prompt: Lines y = 4x + 9 and y = −2x − 3 intersect. Find the x-coordinate of the intersection.
Lines y = 4x + 9 and y = −2x − 3 intersect. Find the y-coordinate of the intersection.
Find the x-coordinate where y = 2x − 5 meets y = −3x + 10.
A cost line C = 5n + 200 and revenue line R = 9n meet at the break-even point. Find n (solve 9n = 5n + 200).
Predicting a value INSIDE the range of the collected data is called:
questions.explain: Predicting within the data range is interpolation; outside it is extrapolation.

questions.prompt: A prediction made well beyond the range of the data (extrapolation) is generally considered less ______ than interpolation.
questions.explain: Extrapolation is less reliable because the trend may not continue beyond the data.
